tweet.text:
Thinking Machines just dropped the best open weight AI model outside of China!

Inkling beats Nemotron 3 Ultra and benchmarks put it between Kimi 2.5 & 2.6. Many were contending to this throne, but Thinky has come out on top.

Really solid release, and will pair well with Tinker.

Artemis x Visa: Agentic Payments from the Ground Up - A Guide to the Current State of Agentic Payments.
> Agentic commerce is gaining traction via x402 and MPP for autonomous micropayments.
> The protocols solve for speed, programmability, and cost. However, scaling requires trust infrastructure, compliance frameworks, merchant distribution, and dispute resolution that incumbent payment players have spent decades building.
> That gap defines the opportunity, and Visa has positioned itself across both models.
> This report examines the protocols, standards, data, and market structure forming around agentic payments today + the opportunities & challenges.
Source:
https://visa.com/api/image-proxy?path=%2Fcontent%2Fdam%2Fvisa%2Freimagine-visa%2Fthought-leadership%2Fdocuments%2Fagentic-payments-report.pdf

github.com/merit-systems/agentcash-router
Source:
https://x.com/OnchainLu/status/2077419510332592457?s=20

Artificial Intelligence

Anthropic
Introduces Claude for Teachers

AI is useful for prep, not the trust layer
This screenshot is a good example of how far a social prompt can go. The model is told to write like a real person, use posts, comments, likes, user behavior, and demographic context, then keep the reply short and conversational.
That is useful for drafting. It gets much shakier once AI starts deciding what feels natural, what deserves a reply, or how to shape the tone around a person’s identity signals.
For founder growth on X, the split is pretty clear:
• Let AI search and cluster
• Let AI draft and summarize
• Keep the thesis, judgment, and final edit human
That’s the trust layer this playbook is really built around.
Data source:
Green Dots Research
Related read:
How to Grow a Founder Persona on X: A Practical Founder Growth System

Crypto Infrastructure.text:
Base's Jesse Pollak Admits Social Bet Was Wrong, Hands Base App Leadership to Cobie
> Base lead Jesse Pollak publicly admits his 2024–2025 bet on onchain social (Farcaster, creator coins) was wrong, acknowledging Base fell behind in perps and prediction markets as a result.
> Now focused on making Base the blockchain for global finance, handing the Base app to Cobie/Coinbase
> Targeting three 2026 pillars: winning trading, payments, and agents.
Source:
https://x.com/jessepollak/status/2077427261586997745?s=20

tweet.text:
lots of conversations about base over the last week. wanted to share my candid take after a week of listening and a lot of reflection over the last 6 months.

first off - in case it’s not obvious, the first quarter of 2026 was a punch in the face. I spent 2024 and 2025 making a two pronged bet to bring base to the world: (1) builders would unlock the next wave of crypto adoption; (2) adoption would be driven by new onchain-native social experiences - creators, content, messaging. imo we made the right bet on builders, but obviously the wrong bet on social. builders did drive the next wave of crypto adoption - prediction markets, perpetuals, stablecoins - but social was not at the center of it. in fact, the entire social side of the market that many of us had been building towards - farcaster, zora, miniapps, and yes, creator coins - disintegrated completely. I was wrong - whether it was timing wrong (is $ansem a creator coin?) or fully wrong, only time will tell, but regardless, i was

Crypto Infrastructure.text: LayerZero Executor Wallets Compromised, $2.4M Drained Across Across Multiple Blockchains  > Attacker has bridged the stolen funds to Ethereum, 956 ETH ($1.79M) and $322K USDC.  Source: https://x.com/PeckShieldAlert/status/2077315396802429254

tweet.text:
Earlier today (12+ hours ago) the LZ executor performed a completely normal course of business rebalancing activity. This was bizarrely interpreted by a smaller X account as a possible exploit and then misreported by many major outlets (without reaching out to us and without significant research into it) including @coinminutes_en @coinbureau @marketsalpha @Cointelegraph 

I would respectfully ask that each outlet who reported on this post a correction, remove these posts, and in general do better with this sort of sensationalist reporting.

Crypto Infrastructure.text: LayerZero Executor Wallets Compromised, $2.4M Drained Across Across Multiple Blockchains  > Attacker has bridged the stolen funds to Ethereum, 956 ETH ($1.79M) and $322K USDC.  Source: https://x.com/PeckShieldAlert/status/2077315396802429254

tweet.text: Earlier today, as part of standard inventory operations, funds were withdrawn from LayerZero internal wallets. No funds are at risk.

LayerZero Executor Wallets Compromised, $2.4M Drained Across Across Multiple Blockchains
> Attacker has bridged the stolen funds to Ethereum, 956 ETH ($1.79M) and $322K USDC.
Source:
https://x.com/PeckShieldAlert/status/2077315396802429254

tweet.text:
#PeckShieldAlert Specter has reported that @LayerZero_Core Executor wallets appear to have been compromised, resulting in $2.4M worth of crypto drained across multiple chains, including #BNBChain, #Base, #Arbitrum, #Avalanche, #Optimism, #Mantle, #Plasma, and #Ethereum. 

The attacker has since bridged the stolen funds to Ethereum, swapping most of it into 956 $ETH ($1.79M) and $322K USDC.

tweet.text:
Threadguy says memecoins are the only thing that brings people back every cycle

“It's the only crypto product that isn't an exchange or a DeFi swap that's gonna survive multi-cycle. It's insane. It's like the only thing that brings people back every time”

“2023, Base launches a chain, nobody cares. Coinbase, the biggest exchange in the world, nobody cares. How do they get people on it? Meme coins”

“2026, three years later after the death three times over, Robinhood, the biggest retail exchange in the world, 27,000,000 funded accounts, wants to tokenize every asset in the world, launches a chain. Nobody cares. How are we getting people on? Meme coins. Every single time”

“It's the only thing crypto people care about, they want to trade meme coins”

tweet.text:
You want to stop getting hacked? 

Kill instant settlement. 

It’s just not worth it.

Every exchange and protocol should add mandatory withdrawal delays.

People will hate it. People might dunk on me for saying this, but these people don’t care about you or the safety of your funds. 

At what point is enough, enough?

(Sending nothing but love out to the Ostium team as they work through this.)

tweet.text:
the new interface is up at

fun.noxa.eth

you can access it directly via brave built-in ens support

or via services like eth limo/link

https://fun.noxa.eth.limo
https://fun.noxa.eth.link

the interface lets you browse a historical snapshot of the tokens launched on noxa in the past, and claim any creator fees, as well as seeing the tokens you launched or where you are the fee receiver

we have pondered for long on the matters of token vamps, and the new token launch spam that has been going on

if crypto as a space has to move forward and improve, the change needs to come from the people

the biggest coins currently present on robinhood, the staples that made the chain what it has become today, even if still in its infancy

people loved the cat, it has been liberated

the legacy of the degens will stay forever

there is only one solution to avoid dilution of the tokens, the staples

and that is to keep new launches disabled

the noxa infrastructure was also never built to sustain suc

I spent 2024 and 2025 making a two pronged bet to bring base to the world: (1) builders would unlock the next wave of crypto adoption; (2) adoption would be driven by new onchain-native social experiences - creators, content, messaging. imo we made the right bet on builders, but obviously the wrong bet on social. builders did drive the next wave of crypto adoption - prediction markets, perpetuals, stablecoins - but social was not at the center of it. in fact, the entire social side of the market that many of us had been building towards - farcaster, zora, miniapps, and yes, creator coins - disintegrated completely. I was wrong - whether it was timing wrong (is $ansem a creator coin?) or fully wrong, only time will tell, but regardless, i was

Prediction Markets.text:
Artemis x Visa: Agentic Payments from the Ground Up - A Guide to the Current State of Agentic Payments.
> Agentic commerce is gaining traction via x402 and MPP for autonomous micropayments.
> The protocols solve for speed, programmability, and cost. However, scaling requires trust infrastructure, compliance frameworks, merchant distribution, and dispute resolution that incumbent payment players have spent decades building.
> That gap defines the opportunity, and Visa has positioned itself across both models.
> This report examines the protocols, standards, data, and market structure forming around agentic payments today + the opportunities & challenges.
Source:
https://visa.com/api/image-proxy?path=%2Fcontent%2Fdam%2Fvisa%2Freimagine-visa%2Fthought-leadership%2Fdocuments%2Fagentic-payments-report.pdf

Ryan Watkins: Crypto Is Entering Its Biggest Adoption Phase
> Ryan Watkins argues blockchains are becoming the global API for money and finance, set to power stablecoins, tokenized equities, perps, prediction markets, onchain credit, and more.
> Bringing crypto to the masses inclusive of humans and agents, with effort to come from both crypto-native apps (e.g Phantom and Fomo) and incumbents like Robinhood and Stripe.
> “Time to look past any month-to-month volatility and focus on the bigger picture unfolding over coming quarters and years…Time to lock in.”
Source:
https://x.com/RyanWatkins_/status/2075595100789318068

tweet.text:
Blockchains are a global API for money and finance.

We’re going to plug this API into every app where it makes sense over the coming years and bring crypto to the masses inclusive of humans and agents.

Stablecoin payments + savings, tokenized assets, onchain credit markets, perps for everything, prediction markets… the list goes on.

All these products are composable, so builders will only be limited by their imaginations on what this could all look like.

Some efforts will be homegrown like Phantom and Fomo.

Others will be established players like Robinhood and Stripe plugging it in and championing this technology.

In any case we will win.

Raises/Funding.text:
Crypto VC funding is still selective
The cleanest signal is the 2021–2022 peak: quarterly capital reached roughly
$12B
, while deal count went above
1,300
. Since then, the market cooled hard. Q1 2026 is still well below that cycle, even after the 2025 rebound.
• Q1 2026: roughly $4B invested across 355 deals
• Q1 2022 peak: about $12B and 1,300+ deals
• 2025 brought a capital spike, but not a matching deal-count recovery
That’s the kind of funding backdrop that makes marketing budgets more defensive: less slack, more scrutiny, and more pressure to prove conversion. It fits the shift toward proof-driven distribution.
Data source:
Galaxy Research
Related read:
Web3 Marketing in 2027: Adapting to bear market budgets

CXMT (ChangXin Memory Technologies) listed on Hyperliquid pre-IPO perps
• China's SK Hynix, China’s largest and the world’s fourth-largest DRAM manufacture (~ 7-9% global DRAM market share)
• Targeted for a July 27th IPO on the Shanghai Stock Exchange STAR Market
• One of Asia’s largest IPOs of 2026 and China’s biggest semiconductor listing in years
• The company received heavy state backing in the form of capital, land, policy support, and alignment with national semiconductor self-reliance goals (part of broader “Made in China 2025” and dual-circulation strategies)
• IPO proceeds are earmarked for further fab expansion, capacity ramps through 2026-2028, and technology upgrades aimed at AI server applications.
SOURCE

Daily news snapshot: Altcoins watchlist
1. Aave (AAVE): +5.8% (24h)
• Market cap: $12.4 billion
• Aave is benefiting from the UK's new capital gains tax deferral for DeFi lending positions and its adoption of Chainlink CCIP as default cross-chain infrastructure.
Matters because:
The mix of policy support and infrastructure upgrades is a constructive signal for DeFi lending adoption.
2. Chainlink (LINK): +4.2% (24h)
• Market cap: $18.7 billion
• Chainlink won major institutional integrations, including U.S. Department of Commerce data feeds, Central Bank of Brazil-HKMA settlement pilots, and Mantle's $2.5B CCIP migration.
• Active network wallets hit an all-time high of 900,000.
Matters because:
Institutional usage and wallet growth suggest accumulation is happening even while price remains suppressed.
3. Solana (SOL): +3.1% (24h)
• Market cap: $89.3 billion
• Solana apps generated record $17M in weekly revenue and set a new $4.15B 24h DEX volume record.
• SBI Holdings launched a Japan-focused tokenization partnership, SBI Solana Global.
Matters because:
The revenue and volume records reinforce Solana's lead in onchain activity and institutional tokenization narratives.

The Residency Opens Applications for Physical AI & Robotics Founders Cohort  > The Residency is seeking and backing founders building durable physical AI and robotics startups focused on solving deep technical problems  To apply
👉
apply.livetheresidency.com/apply…

The Beginner’s Guide to Physical AI Stack
Physical AI encompasses industrial robots, warehouse automation, and autonomous vehicles
The 5 layer stack: Brain (AI/VLA models), Body (humanoid), Parts (actuators, gearboxes, sensors), Fuel (training data via teleoperation, simulation, and fleet experience), and Proving Grounds (actual deployments)
https://x.com/muskan_kalra24/status/2077090406819647871?s=20

Nobody is close to breaking ECC
Although theoretical advances have dramatically reduced the quantum hardware needed to break Elliptic Curve Cryptography, current quantum computers are still orders of magnitude away from having enough error-corrected qubits to execute the attack
Consequently, standard internet protocols and cryptocurrencies face no immediate risk, though exposed public addresses will eventually need to transition to quantum-resistant encryption
https://derrickcui.substack.com/p/nobody-is-close-to-breaking-ecc

Robotics.text:
data refineries: turning abundant data into trustworthy, actionable intelligence
this is the moat
https://x.com/michellelsun/status/2076705184898785637

tweet.text:
Data volume is no longer the bottleneck for robotics training. 

It’s building dynamic pipelines - like oil refineries in the AI age - that guarantee trustworthy lineage + usable signal for training and deployment.

A single robot spits out terabytes of raw camera, LiDAR & IMU streams daily. 

Training data supply is exploding globally, especially with the surge of data collection startups across India, China, and beyond.

In order for these data to be useful, we need robust processing pipelines that handle : 
- Provenance tracking (where did this frame come from? which embodiment? under what conditions?)
- Cleaning, synchronization, labeling
- Repeatedly & reliably turning it into high-quality multi-modal tokens for training and real-time deployment

The new moat in robotics is the robust, dynamic data infrastructure that turns raw observations into trustworthy, actionable intelligence.

Who’s building the best data refineries?

Security/Exploits.text: LayerZero Executor Wallets Compromised, $2.4M Drained Across Across Multiple Blockchains  > Attacker has bridged the stolen funds to Ethereum, 956 ETH ($1.79M) and $322K USDC.  Source: https://x.com/PeckShieldAlert/status/2077315396802429254

tweet.text:
Earlier today (12+ hours ago) the LZ executor performed a completely normal course of business rebalancing activity. This was bizarrely interpreted by a smaller X account as a possible exploit and then misreported by many major outlets (without reaching out to us and without significant research into it) including @coinminutes_en @coinbureau @marketsalpha @Cointelegraph 

I would respectfully ask that each outlet who reported on this post a correction, remove these posts, and in general do better with this sort of sensationalist reporting.

You Just Hired a Million Bad Employees.
> “AI was supposed to replace human labor. It did the opposite. For the first time in history, humans are cheaper than software. And AI is creating more jobs than it eliminates."
> However, wasted tokens are the new headcount bloat. As most firms have thin layer of doers at the bottom, with the rest of the org in management.
> Token usage follows a similar structure, with 10% of tokens shipping useful output, and 90% wasted on unproductive loops.
> This piece discuss what the core lessons of human management mean for agent workforces
👇
Source:
https://x.com/gsivulka/status/2077070925154161101

Nobody is close to breaking ECC
Although theoretical advances have dramatically reduced the quantum hardware needed to break Elliptic Curve Cryptography, current quantum computers are still orders of magnitude away from having enough error-corrected qubits to execute the attack
Consequently, standard internet protocols and cryptocurrencies face no immediate risk, though exposed public addresses will eventually need to transition to quantum-resistant encryption
https://derrickcui.substack.com/p/nobody-is-close-to-breaking-ecc

CXMT (ChangXin Memory Technologies) listed on Hyperliquid pre-IPO perps
• China's SK Hynix, China’s largest and the world’s fourth-largest DRAM manufacture (~ 7-9% global DRAM market share)
• Targeted for a July 27th IPO on the Shanghai Stock Exchange STAR Market
• One of Asia’s largest IPOs of 2026 and China’s biggest semiconductor listing in years
• The company received heavy state backing in the form of capital, land, policy support, and alignment with national semiconductor self-reliance goals (part of broader “Made in China 2025” and dual-circulation strategies)
• IPO proceeds are earmarked for further fab expansion, capacity ramps through 2026-2028, and technology upgrades aimed at AI server applications.
SOURCE
